title: Industry Storyboard
description: Single-step LLM reasoning artifact that merges storyline strategy, page planning, page type selection, slide-level PPT copy, and template binding for the IB Industry Section workflow.

section_meta.description: Top-level metadata for this industry section generation run.

industry.description: Industry classification (e.g., 'China Premium Tea-based Beverage Chain').

output_mode.description: Output mode. Currently only standard_8_slide is supported.

source_memo.description: Path to the canonical industry_input_memo.md used as input.

storyline_strategy.description: Narrative strategy that governs the entire 8-slide section. This is where LLM reasoning about the industry thesis lives.

one_sentence_thesis.description: The single most important conclusion this industry section should leave the reader with.

transaction_relevance.description: Why this industry story matters for the transaction (IPO, M&A, fundraising, etc.).

primary_investor_questions.description: The 3-5 most important questions an investor would ask about this industry in the context of this transaction.

key_messages.description: The 5-8 key messages that the 8-slide section must convey. Each message should map to roughly one slide.

known_weaknesses_or_data_gaps.description: Known limitations: data that could not be verified, segments with weak sourcing, or analytical gaps.

tone_guidance.description: Tone direction: bullish, balanced, cautious, defensive, etc., with rationale.

slides.description: The 8-slide page plan. Each slide includes narrative role, page type selection with rationale, and PPT-ready copy.

slide_story_contract.description: Per-slide planning contract filled BEFORE copy drafting. Ensures each slide has a single clear story and MECE boundaries.

question.description: The single investor question this slide answers. One question only.

answer.description: One-sentence conclusion that directly answers the question. This should align with the headline.

evidence_ids.description: Evidence IDs (e.g., EV-001) from the memo that support this slide's answer.

forbidden_topics.description: Content types that must NOT appear on this slide to maintain MECE boundaries. E.g., for Slide 3: ['CR5/CR10', 'channel structure', 'value chain'].

visual_role.description: What the visual area should communicate. E.g., 'Show market size growth over 3 years as a bar chart'.

selected_page_type.description: The page type chosen for this slide. Must be a valid option per templates/page_type_rules.json.

decision_rationale.description: Why this page type was chosen over alternatives. Required for controlled-variant slides (2, 6, 7); for fixed slides, explain the content-structure fit.

headline.description: Conclusion-led slide headline. Must be a complete investment insight, not a topic label.

main_message.description: One-sentence takeaway that captures the slide's core argument.

body_copy.description: Slide body content structured for PPT. Fields vary by page type. Use the field names expected by ppt_copy_schema.json for this slide role.

visual_direction.description: Guidance for chart, diagram, or visual element. What should the visual communicate? What data should drive it?

chart_data.description: Optional structured data payload for chart-ready slides. Use when the slide depends on a quantitative visual rather than only a qualitative sketch.

chart_type.description: Suggested visual type such as bar, line, stacked_bar, matrix, waterfall, or table.

x_axis_label.description: Optional x-axis label, especially for matrix visuals.

y_axis_label.description: Optional y-axis label, especially for matrix visuals.

categories.description: X-axis categories, time periods, segments, or buckets.

unit.description: Display unit for values, such as RMB bn, %, stores, or index.

source_rows.description: Traceable source rows or datapoints used to construct the chart. For matrix_page, rows may include numeric x and y values or a value object/list containing x/y coordinates.

notes.description: Construction notes, caveats, or interpolation logic.

target_link.description: Explicit connection to the target company. Every slide must answer: why does this matter for the target?

source_note.description: Source attribution for facts on this slide. Reference memo section or specific source name.

data_gaps.description: Known data gaps or unverified claims on this slide.

template_binding.description: Final decisions on which template variant to use for controlled-variant slides.

slide_2_variant.description: Selected variant for Slide 2 (market size & segmentation).

slide_3_variant.description: Selected variant for Slide 3 (key industry drivers).

inactive_variants_to_remove.description: List of page types NOT selected on variant slides. Used by clean_filled_ppt.py to remove physical slides.

qc_self_check.description: Self-assessment of the storyboard quality before human review.

generic_industry_report_risk.description: Does this read like a generic industry report? If so, what's missing to make it transaction-specific?

target_linkage_check.description: Does every slide explicitly connect back to the target? Flag any slide where the connection is weak.

source_support_check.description: Are all key numbers sourced? Are any facts fabricated or unverifiable? List concerns.

page_repetition_check.description: Is any content repeated across slides? Do adjacent slides have distinct messages?

template_fit_check.description: Will the copy fit in the PPT template? Are any slides overloaded with text?
